definition: Statistically recurring patterns tied to calendar periods — certain months, weeks, or events produce predictable directional biases.
related_to: quarterly_shifts (seasonal patterns coincide with quarterly boundaries)

EURUSD.notes: Jan: New year USD weakness. Mar: Quarter-end flows. Sep: European Q3 repatriation. Jun/Nov: Risk-off USD strength phases.

XAUUSD.notes: Jan-Feb: New year safe-haven demand, Chinese New Year buying. Sep-Oct: Pre-Q4 safe haven positioning. Jun-Jul: Summer risk-on reduces gold demand.

US_indices.weak: September is historically the worst month for equities (S&P 500 averages negative returns).
US_indices.strong: November through April — the 'best 6 months' seasonal window. Santa rally in December is well-documented.
US_indices.sell_in_may: May through October historically underperforms. Reduce equity conviction during this window.
US_indices.notes: October can be volatile (capitulation lows before Q4 rally). April is often strong (tax refund season retail buying).

BTC_crypto.halving_exception: Post-halving years (2013, 2017, 2021, 2025) see Q2-Q3 weakness replaced by extended bull phases. The halving cycle overrides seasonal tendencies.
BTC_crypto.notes: BTC January typically bullish (new year FOMO). September weakest historically. December sees year-end positioning.

weekly_patterns.monday: Range building. Institutional desks re-open after weekend. Low conviction directional movement. Often retraces Friday's close move. Avoid new directional entries.
weekly_patterns.tuesday: Continuation or reversal setup day. Monday's range is tested. Tuesday often establishes the weekly bias direction. Watch for Tuesday reversal patterns when Monday was a strong directional day.
weekly_patterns.wednesday: HIGHEST VOLATILITY DAY. Most common day for weekly manipulation sweep. Wednesday sweeps Monday's high or Monday's low approximately 60-70% of the time. This IS the weekly manipulation day.
weekly_patterns.thursday: Distribution day. After Wednesday's manipulation, Thursday delivers the real directional weekly move. Highest conviction entry day.
weekly_patterns.friday: Reduced volume and position squaring. Institutions reduce exposure ahead of weekend. Avoid new entries. Best used to close existing positions from Thursday's distribution move.

intraday_seasonal.930_1030_ET: Highest volume window of the day. NY open establishes the intraday direction. The 9:30-10:30 candle is the highest probability manipulation or distribution candle. Never miss this window.
intraday_seasonal.1030_1200_ET: Continuation or first retracement. Volume drops slightly. Good for scaling into Thursday/Friday continuation moves.
intraday_seasonal.1200_1300_ET: Lunch doldrums. Volume drops sharply. Spreads widen. Avoid new entries. This is a dead zone for intraday trading.
intraday_seasonal.1300_1530_ET: PM session push. Institutions re-enter. Second high-conviction window of day, especially on trending days. 2:00-2:30 ET often sees a directional push before CBDR begins.
intraday_seasonal.1530_1600_ET: Market-on-close (MOC) orders execute. Final 30 minutes can be chaotic — institutional position management for EOD.

patterns.january_effect: Equities tend bullish early January (new year allocation)
patterns.summer_doldrums: Jul-Aug: Lower volatility, reduced institutional activity, VIX tends lower
patterns.Q4_rally: Oct-Dec: Institutional window dressing, holiday rally tendency
patterns.NFP_week: First Friday of month: increased volatility around Non-Farm Payrolls
patterns.FOMC_drift: Pre-FOMC drift upward is statistically documented in equities

bridge_usage.conviction_increase: During historically strong months for the instrument being traded, add +5 confluence points to any setup that already passes the standard validation threshold.
bridge_usage.conviction_decrease: During summer doldrums (July-August) and September for equities, reduce position size by 25%. Seasonal headwinds reduce follow-through probability.
bridge_usage.doldrum_filter: Jul-Aug: Accept only A+ setups (highest confluence). Reject B and C grade setups. Lower activity = lower reliability of intraday patterns.
bridge_usage.high_probability_windows: During the best 6 months window (Nov-Apr for equities), lower the confluence threshold by 1 criterion. The seasonal tailwind compensates for slightly reduced technical confirmation.
bridge_usage.quarterly_rebalancing_edge: End of quarter months (March, June, September, December) — the last 2 weeks often see institutional rebalancing flows that create predictable mean-reversion moves. Add +5 points if setup aligns with expected rebalancing direction.

quarterly_shift_rules.definition: Every 13 weeks (quarter end: March, June, September, December), institutional portfolios rebalance. This creates predictable price behavior.
quarterly_shift_rules.last_2_weeks_of_quarter: Institutional rebalancing creates directional flows as funds adjust allocations. The dominant direction of the prior quarter often reverses in the last 2 weeks as profits are taken.
quarterly_shift_rules.first_2_weeks_of_new_quarter: New quarter often sees the prior quarter's trend initially reversed as the IPDA resets its 20/40/60-day lookback windows. This is the quarterly shift — expect counter-trend moves in early January, April, July, October.
quarterly_shift_rules.week_3_of_new_quarter: By week 3 of a new quarter, the new quarterly trend direction is usually established. This is when trend-following setups resume maximum conviction.

event_calendar_integration.NFP: Non-Farm Payrolls — first Friday of each month. Extreme volatility. Spreads widen. Stop hunting is aggressive. Avoid entries 30 minutes before and during NFP. Post-NFP (15+ minutes after) can offer high conviction continuation or reversal setups.
event_calendar_integration.CPI: Consumer Price Index — mid-month (usually 2nd Wednesday). USD-affecting. Treat like NFP for risk management. Wait for the initial spike and reversal before entering.
event_calendar_integration.FOMC: Federal Open Market Committee — 8 meetings per year. Highest impact single event. The week of FOMC, reduce position size 50%. The FOMC drift (pre-meeting drift up in equities) is well-documented — do not fade it.
event_calendar_integration.OpEx: Options Expiration — third Friday of each month. Maximum pain pinning creates reduced ranges the week of OpEx. Breakouts during OpEx week are unreliable. Post-OpEx Monday often sees a relief move.
event_calendar_integration.quarter_end_rebalancing: Last trading day of each quarter — extreme institutional flows. Vol spikes, spreads widen. Avoid trading the final hour of quarter-end.
event_calendar_integration.calendar_rule: Know the economic calendar for the week before taking swing positions. NFP and FOMC weeks require reduced size and tighter management regardless of how perfect the technical setup is.

how_to_use: Seasonal tendencies are CONTEXT, not signals. They increase/decrease conviction for trades already validated by layers 1-5. Never trade solely on seasonality.
